content: Just as the linguistic concept of reflexion (as both physical reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection) is taught as a core literacy skill to help high school students refine their own writing by stepping back to critique their work, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this literacy framework to structure graded reflection training: first mimicking basic 'reflecting output back to input' (mirroring the physical reflection meaning) before moving to deliberate critical self-critique (mirroring the cognitive reflection meaning). This two-stage framing makes LLM reflection training more accessible to transfer to educational fine-tuning for student-facing AI tutors.
